title: Is Nausicaa the inspiration for the evas
display_title: Is Nausicaa the inspiration for the evas
wikitext:
[]: Is Nausicaa the inspiration for the evas?             Quote Post by Desty_Nova » Thu Aug 19, 2010 6:51 am Perhaps it's already been covered, I had a picture to go with this but I'm posting from a phone and this forum doesn't seem to allow attachments? Anyway, the picture is of the giant warrior skeleton which appears during the nausicaa end credits, this version of the skeleton has a far more artificial look than what we see during the film which suggests to me that the giant warriors were manufactured by someone- possibly humans. Perhaps this was covered in the manga or in interviews with miyazaki but the idea of the giants being artificial never crossed my mind until just now. As you all know anno was involved in the creation/animation of the giant warriors so can anyone fill me in on the relationship between the evas and annos work with miyazaki?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Aug 21, 2010 4:08 pm Check the wiki. I thought there was a thread related to this on another evageeks subforum, but if there is, I can't find it... There are more similarities between NGE and the Nausicaa comic due to how expanded the storyline and themes are compared to the film, but the god warrior is certainly an aspect present in both. I highly recommend looking into the manga, since it seems like you haven't had the chance to yet.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Aug 23, 2010 12:43 pm The manga for Nausicaa is really good and while it does get sort of Evangelion-like in some parts, its a completely different story with vastly differing themes and characters.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Mon Aug 23, 2010 9:32 pm True, but that's only to be expected given that they were created by different authors with different stories in mind. The fact is that Anno actually drew the giant warrior revival scene, a story which he apparently used to woo the ladies several times (unsuccessfully!) afterwards =D

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Aug 26, 2010 7:34 am If it wasn't for Nausicaä manga Neon Genesis Evangelion wouldn't exist. AyrYntake wrote: The fact is that Anno actually drew the giant warrior revival scene, a story which he apparently used to woo the ladies several times (unsuccessfully!) afterwards =D

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Aug 27, 2010 3:57 am Fact. I read it in a book somewhere, it must be true. XD
